findings.summary: PaymentGatewayTimeoutError in payment_processor Lambda. Traceback: File "src/payment_processor/handler.py", line 73, in _process_batch: _charge_payment(txn["transaction_id"], txn["amount"], txn["currency"]). File "src/payment_processor/handler.py", line 65, in _charge_payment: return _gateway_client_post(...). File "src/payment_processor/handler.py", line 57, in _gateway_client_post: raise PaymentGatewayTimeoutError("Gateway timeout after 30000ms — upstream payment provider api.stripe-mock.example.com not responding")
Error rate for payment_processor spiked to 100% at 09:05 UTC. 3 of 10 transactions succeeded before failure.

hypotheses.summary: The payment gateway (api.stripe-mock.example.com) is timing out after 30s. The _process_batch function has no error handling for individual transaction failures, causing the entire batch to abort when one gateway call fails.
CHAOS_MODE environment variable may be enabled, triggering intentional failure on the 4th transaction in _process_batch.

next_actions.summary: Add try/except around _charge_payment calls in _process_batch to handle individual transaction failures gracefully
Add retry logic with exponential backoff in _gateway_client_post for transient gateway timeouts
